data.title: Horizon Quantum and Alice & Bob collaborate to advance fault-tolerant quantum computing, boosting software development.
data.description: A collaboration has been announced between Horizon Quantum Computing Pte. Ltd. and Alice & Bob to enhance the development of fault-tolerant quantum computing software. Horizon Quantum specializes in software infrastructure for quantum applications while Alice & Bob is known for its fault-tolerant quantum computers. This partnership aims to combine their expertise and streamline the deployment of FTQC software. Through integrating A&B’s emulators with Triple Alpha, the collaboration seeks to advance quantum computing technology.

data.title: Heineken N.V. updates on transactions in its ongoing share buyback scheme.
data.description: A new report reveals that the unemployment rate in the United States reached 6.2% last month, with a total of 10.1 million Americans currently unemployed. This marks an improvement from the previous month when the rate was at 6.7%. Additionally, the report shows that the economy added 379,000 jobs in February, exceeding expectations. The strong job growth was primarily seen in the leisure and hospitality, education, and construction sectors. Despite this positive news, there are still concerns about the long-term impact of the ongoing pandemic on the job market. Economists are closely monitoring the situation as the country continues its recovery efforts.

data.title: Mullen Group Ltd. reveals 2026 business plan post 2025 update, approved by Board of Directors.
data.description: Mullen Group Ltd., a company based in Okotoks, Alberta and listed on the TSX under the ticker symbol MTL, disclosed that its 2026 plan has been given the green light by the Board of Directors. The announcement was made on January 19, 2026.
Company sees increase in sales again.

data.title: Cerrado Gold concludes gold hedging program, now selling 100% of production at near-spot prices, proposes normal course issuer bid.
data.description: The company has successfully hedged its gold, ensuring the sale of 100% of its production at prices closely aligned with current market rates. Plans for a Normal Course Issuer Bid have also been put forth by the company.

data.title: LibertyStream increases non-brokered LIFE offering to $12,500,000 due to high demand for units at $1.10 each.
data.description: Dallas-- LibertyStream Infrastructure Partners Inc. has recently announced an upsized non-brokered private placement offering of units at $1.10 per Unit, originally declared on January 16, 2026. In response to high investor interest, the Offering has been raised by $2,500,000, increasing the total proceeds from $10,000,000 to an undisclosed new limit. LibertyStream is listed on TSXV as LIB, OTCQB as VLTLF, and FSE as I2D.
